methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe for this stage and subsector is limited. Eight private companies operate in the broader Drones & Autonomous space, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public companies trade in this narrow category, constraining traditional public market benchmarking.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains five same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints in each category—below the minimum threshold for reliable median analysis. Attempting to use an all-sector median would introduce significant methodological distortion given the heterogeneity of valuation drivers across different autonomous technology categories.

The analysis lacks sufficient comparable data to support a triangulated valuation range at this time. To establish credible benchmarks for Airship Industries, the following uncertainties would need to be resolved: the company's specific subsegment within drones and autonomous systems (cargo delivery, surveillance, infrastructure, etc.), the capital intensity and development stage of comparable peers, and the availability of transaction multiples or fundraising data from earlier-stage competitors. Additional primary research or data enrichment on the five identified peer companies would be necessary before advancing to valuation synthesis.
